title: Clean Fuels Alliance America Applauds CARBâ€™s Decision to Sunset Outdated Biodiesel Restrictions
content: Clean Fuels Alliance America has applauded the California Air Resources Boardâ€™s (CARB) decision to sunset the oxides of nitrogen (NOx) mitigation requirement for biodiesel blends up to B20 under the Alternative Diesel Fuel (ADF) regulation, effective January 1, 2026. Previously, regulations required producers to mix at least 55% renewable diesel with biodiesel blends between B6 and B20 to address emissions in older heavy-duty vehicles. Clean Fuels had advocated for this change since 2022, noting that CARBâ€™s own data confirmed these restrictions were no longer necessary. This decision removes a significant barrier that had kept overall biodiesel blends below 10%, offering a cost-effective compliance option for fleet operators. Jeffrey Earl, Director of State Governmental Affairs, called the move a constructive step that provides regulatory clarity, though the organization continues to challenge other recent CARB actions to ensure policies remain grounded in sound data and affordability.
author: Analysts Account
